summary: Sovereign AI for most countries will be fine-tuned open-source models run in local data centers, not frontier model development; China will fall further behind
summary_extended: Baker argues that sovereign AI for virtually all countries outside the US (and possibly China) will not involve frontier model development, but rather a practical stack: fine-tuning open-source models on local language, culture, and values using RL from a provider, adding system prompts and supervised fine-tuning, and running the result in local data centers. He views frontier sovereign AI as structurally non-viable for most nations due to the prohibitive cost and talent requirements. On China specifically, Baker calls their strategy a 'terrible mistake' — relying on domestic chips and distillation of Western frontier models is a dead end because as frontier models get locked down (e.g., via initiatives like Mythos), China's distillation advantage disappears and they fall further behind. The investment implication is macro-structural rather than single-stock: the thesis favors open-source model ecosystems and local inference infrastructure, but no specific US-listed equity is the unambiguous beneficiary given the diffuse, geopolitical nature of the claim.
detail: Baker's view: sovereign AI for all countries except US (and possibly China) will be: use a provider for RL on local language/culture/values, add system prompt and supervised fine-tuning, run on best open-source model in local data centers. Frontier sovereign AI is not viable. China is making a 'terrible mistake' relying on domestic chips and distillation of Western models; as frontier models get locked down (e.g., Mythos), China's distillation advantage disappears.
confidence_evidence: Baker states 'I think that's where sovereign AI ends up' and 'sovereign AI at the frontier I don't see it' with high conviction; provides structural reasoning
quote_fragment: sovereign AI at the frontier I don't see it
tickers_implied_original: local data center operators in non-US/China countries
